This is another lovable-like app builder thing.

They advertise: "But the problem is when it breaks, you're staring at code
you can't read, hoping someone can fix it. Bubble
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/company/bubble/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!V93LsQnQLEzPrd-uRB74K3GpR2KCW8TGcItuM_7Qb8219ywBRkBR9_eZmA0F68PGp8kTiYsOCK1Q-YTivC1QL-wQ8jCu$ >'s approach is different. Their
AI Agent builds visually, so you can actually see what it did. No mystery
code. No cleanup crew needed. You can edit it yourself because you
understand what you're looking at."

I'm not sure exactly what it does, but I resonate with the idea of making
it easier to understand the code AI puts out.

emails.subject: guidelines from my CC section, and how to connect to hundreds of millions of academic articles
emails.body:
I paste below the guidelines I gave my class

But here's how you can connect it to OpenAlex
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://github.com/nac-codes/open-alex-mcp/blob/main/README.md__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WAdqjTk_7howxz0xlEl3aLk1ZEL7_Bd76DAkBt9rxWf_9uSxdSR9VThJXE54B0-E5C1mJXt8cqhLGoii-otwbxs$ > with
either Claude or ChatGPT (I think you will need a non-free account)

You can ignore the below, but extra credit for whoever come sup with the
best way to hack my efforts to AI-proof writing.

Matt

Valid uses of Ai include searching for sources, translating foreign
language materials, or analyzing technical aspects of your topic that might
otherwise be hard to understand. CUIT
<https://www.cuit.columbia.edu/services#!#%2Fservice%2Fterm%2F183 > now
makes several gen AI tools available for free (or almost free.) I
particularly recommend NotebookLM, since it will only generate answers from
the sources you load into your notebooks. If you use Gemini or CU CHAT (the
Columbia interface for ChatGPT), I recommend that you engineer your prompts
to focus on scholarly literature, provide sources, and double check all
work. If you have a Claude pro account you can connect it to OpenAlex
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://github.com/nac-codes/open-alex-mcp/blob/main/README.md__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WAdqjTk_7howxz0xlEl3aLk1ZEL7_Bd76DAkBt9rxWf_9uSxdSR9VThJXE54B0-E5C1mJXt8cqhLGoii-otwbxs$ >, which
will tap into a repository of hundreds of millions of academic articles.

But no matter what AI tools you use, do not depend on AI to summarize
anything you have not read for yourself. It will misrepresent sources and
invent them outright, in ways that will be invisible to you if you have not
done the work yourself. This can happen even when it produces links to the
sources it used. If you do not double check everything for yourself, the
resulting errors will be your responsibility, and they will seriously
reduce your grade. If it appears you have used AI to substitute for your
own critical reasoning, it will make me skeptical of everything else you
have written.

You may not use AI for any aspect of writing the paper, whether
brain-storming, outlining, drafting, critiquing, or editing. You need to do
all your writing in the same Google Doc. But you should insert document
tabs to separate the different components (topic proposal, lit review,
etc.) You can insert as many tabs as you want, such as to start a new draft
in the same document. Just be sure that you do all your writing and editing
in the same document, and clearly indicate the final version of each
component in the name of the tab (topic proposal, lit review, full paper).

Grammarly

Grammarly is a form of AI, like the built-in spelling and grammar checkers
in Google Docs. All of these tools can be used to highlight spelling and
grammatical errors, which is encouraged. But you should not use them for
any other purpose, such as suggesting ways to improve on your own writing.
Writing is hard work. It is not supposed to be easy. Writing is thinking,
right down to the way you choose particular words to express your precise
meaning. Putting yourself in the position of the reader usually requires
that you think hard about how to make your ideas clear and compelling. You
will not learn how to do this more effectively if you come to depend on
Grammarly or other tools as a crutch.

For that reason, if you plan to use Grammarly, you must configure it
appropriately. So turn off suggestion categories in the Grammarly web
editor, browser extension, or sidebar of Google Docs, i.e. deselect Clarity,
Engagement, and Delivery, and select just Correctness. That way, Grammarly
will mostly flag errors of grammar, spelling, and punctuation, and not
suggest synonyms or rewrites. Even if suggestions pop up, use these tools
only as a highlighter for potential issues, and decide for yourself whether
and how to fix them in your own way (if indeed they need to be fixed).

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Matthew Connelly

Professor

emails.subject: Re: syllabus, and something we will be looking at during our first session
emails.body:
Dear Friends:

Lydia and I are looking forward to seeing you in the Studio this morning.
Please note that, based on our experience last time, we decided to reverse
the topic we will be exploring today. Instead of text and context, we are
going to focus on "Finding Connections." I've pasted the relevant part of
the syllabus below.

Best

Matt

Session 3: Finding Connections (early- to mid-November 2025)

Making meaningful connections between texts is one of the most powerful
ways to deepen understanding and strengthen memory. Drawing links—across
works, authors, disciplines, and traditions—can help us grasp abstract
ideas more concretely, see recurring patterns more clearly, and appreciate
nuance more fully. But our brains often miss these opportunities. Sometimes
we forget what we've read, or focus too narrowly. Sometimes connections
only emerge after repeated encounters or guided reflection.

In this session, students will experiment with using AI to surface
connections between core texts—primarily from Contemporary Civilization,
but also from Literature Humanities, Art Humanities, and Music Humanities.
They will try prompting AI to relate different texts by theme (e.g.,
justice), by intellectual lineage (e.g., Plato and Augustine), by
rhetorical style (e.g., prophetic, dialogic, polemical), or by formal or
aesthetic strategies.

Faculty will support students in critically evaluating AI-generated
connections: Are they substantive or superficial? Surprising or strained?
What interpretive assumptions are embedded in the link? Students will then
practice refining their own prompts to surface more meaningful insights and
reflect on how the process enhances or limits their own capacity for
synthesis. Ultimately, this session asks: What does it mean to read in
conversation—not just with a single author, but with a tradition? And how
can AI, used wisely, help us become more attentive and imaginative readers?

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Matthew Connelly

Professor of History

Vice Dean for AI Initiatives

403 Fayerweather Hall

Department of History

Mail Code 2508

1180 Amsterdam Avenue

Columbia University

New York, NY 10027

(212) 854-4563

is
seeking its next cohort of brilliant minds ready to invent cutting-edge
technologies! Research internships for Summer of 2026 are now open to
applicants pursuing PhD and Master’s degrees in the following research
areas: Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning, AR, VR & 360
Photography, Audio, Computer Vision, Imaging & Video, Content Intelligence,
Data Intelligence, Document Intelligence, Graphics (2D & 3D), Human
Computer Interaction, Intelligent Agents & Assistants, Natural Language
Processing, and Systems & Languages. Our 2026 Summer internships will be
offered as hybrid roles, co-located with Adobe Research mentors.

Interns collaborate directly with one or more Adobe researchers on our
staff as well as with Adobe’s world-class product and design teams. Work by
Adobe researchers and interns is frequently presented at top-tier academic
conferences and may be integrated into Adobe’s products, reaching millions
of users worldwide. Adobe Research is especially interested in fostering
ongoing relationships that last beyond the internship and that may even
contribute to an intern’s PhD thesis.

Let me know if you’re interested!

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Katie Clark <katie.clark2@capitalone.com>
Date: Mon, Sep 22, 2025 at 17:04
Subject: Capital One Applied Research Internship Program | Information
Session
To:
CC: Bari Goldschein <bari.goldschein@capitalone.com>

Hi there, We are thrilled to share with you an invite only virtual
informational event for our Applied Research Internship Program. The event
will take place on September 29 at 3: 30-4: 30pm EST. If you have any
students in mind that you think
ZjQcmQRYFpfptBannerStart
This Message Is From an External Sender
This message came from outside your organization.

ZjQcmQRYFpfptBannerEnd

Hi there,

We are thrilled to share with you an invite only virtual informational
event for our Applied Research Internship Program. The event will take
place on September 29 at 3:30-4:30pm EST. If you have any students in mind
that you think might be a good fit for this program, please send them this
information.

This event will include general information about the program, life at
Capital One, followed by the opportunity to hear from Bayan Bruss
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/jobs/__;!!EFVe01R3CjU!ZkW-SyBWcRd80WDxCG3TLFgZ5FSn7yRqzGDyT-S0RZQC0oCtiaudMJjgW_Ynhdsrj6NuZFfF4iTXxpJOx5_vip0CuFxHitLZXrI$>,
VP, Applied Research. For more information about the event and how to
register, see the attached flyer.

About the Program:

This program is a 12-week PhD-level internship in which interns conduct
high impact applied research within the latest AI developments. Interns
will have the opportunity to work towards publications at top conferences,
access VP level knowledge, and expand their network. As we are at the
beginning of our recruiting cycle for summer 2027, we hope you can join us
next Monday to learn more about the program!

If you have any questions, please reach out to @Bari Goldschein

Thank you!

Katie

--

Katie Clark

Intelligent Foundations & Experiences (IFX) COS
West Creek 4

emails.subject: [tickets.cs.columbia.edu #206975] AutoReply: Request for Poster Reimbursement
emails.body:
Greetings,

This message has been automatically generated in response to your 
Reimbursement Request. For future communications, please reply to this 
email without making any changes to the subject line.

To expedite the payment process, please make sure you submit:

·Overall business purpose of the expense.

·Itemized receipts/invoice *and* proof of payment.

·Provide the project to be charged.

·If you are a student, please let us know the professor responsible for 
the charges.

For all guidelines, forms and other reimbursement information, please 
visit our website.
 
<https://www.cs.columbia.edu/travel-and-business-expense-reimbursement/ >

                                 Thank you,

                            CS Reimbursement Team

                        reimbursement@cs.columbia.edu

-------------------------------------------------------------------------
Hi,

My name is Karthik and I am a PhD student at Columbia in the Computer
Science department advised by Lydia Chilton. Earlier this week, I presented
a poster at the AI Agents for Work workshop
<https://daplab.cs.columbia.edu/workshop/2025-10/  >. I was directed to this
email address by a lab mate of mine to request reimbursement for the poster.

I've attached a screenshot of my order receipt and a screenshot of the
charge on my credit card. Please let me know if there is any other
materials you need, or whether there is another place I should direct my
reimbursement request to. Thanks for your help!

Thanks,
Karthik

emails.body:
I recently submitted a grant for TRI with Andrew Head. Of course, we
waiting until the last minute. Most of our history of collaboration is in
my inbox.

I'd love to be able to 1) find all the emails related to the grant and 2)
analyze them to see how and went we actually did the work... so for next
time I could have a better estimate of the time it takes and our cadence
for working.

Not necessarily an immediate thing, just a goal to keep on the list :)

emails.subject: Re: Thank you! MISQ article submitted.
emails.body:
Glad to be a part of the team! It was fun working in a very different discipline. I’m wishing the little manuscript a safe editing voyage.

Best,
Jocelyn

--

From: Jeff Nickerson <jvnickerson@gmail.com>
Date: Saturday, September 13, 2025 at 4:05 PM
To: Zhang, Shiyan <shiyan.zhang@lancaster.ac.uk>, Jocelyn McKinnon-Crowley <jmckin02@syr.edu>, Alice Cai <caia@mit.edu>, Karthik Sreedhar <ks4190@columbia.edu>, Lydia Chilton <chilton@cs.columbia.edu>
Subject: Thank you! MISQ article submitted.

Jocelyn and Alice, your contributions were quite valuable the last couple of days as Shiyan and I worked against the deadline.

I think it is an interesting manuscript; let's see what the review team says.

Hopefully we get another round; we will doubtless have learned more from the journalists in the meantime.

By the way, yesterday I did a hackathon with Alex (and others from our board game group) sponsored by Anthropic. Every project was about synthetic personae, but oriented to helping early stage startups find customers. There is an ecosystem of products being created for this purpose.

Call/text my cell if you have any trouble finding it :)

510-376-9964

Can't wait to see you again!!!!
lydia

p.s. Here's the syllabus if you can't see it in Courseworks.

About the class

*Digital Storytelling III: Immersive Production* is a mix of theory and
practice. Teams of students work together to design, build, and deploy an
immersive storytelling experience that is staged for the public at the end
of the semester. The class combines project work, mentorship, emerging
technologies, and collaborative methods to create a dynamic, hands-on
experiential learning environment that blends storytelling with code.

For the fall semester, the class will collaboratively design and produce an
immersive experience that embraces speculative design, worldbuilding,
placemaking, and MDA theory. The theme or topic for the immersive
experience will be determined at the start of the semester. In past years,
themes have included de-escalating violence within social media, biases
within AI and algorithms, as well as an Existential Haunted House centered
on the climate crisis.

Finally, the class offers an incredible opportunity to collaborate with
leading industry practitioners working in film, TV, theatre, gaming, and
emergent technology. Guests will join the class as active participants, and
together we will develop the immersive experience that will be staged at
the end of the semester. The course design is similar to the labs I have
helped mentor and/or develop for organizations such as Sundance, Tribeca,
the NFB, and others.

*Learning Goals*
In this course students will:

   -

   *Learn Team-Based Production Techniques:* Students will acquire advanced
   skills in team-based emergent media production, integrating lectures,
   mentoring, and hands-on experience to create a sophisticated immersive
   storytelling project.
   -

   *Enhance Critical Evaluation Skills:* Through regular peer reviews and
   collaborative critiques, students will refine their ability to critically
   assess and improve immersive media projects, focusing on design,
   implementation, and deployment phases.
   -

   *Learn Speculative Design and Worldbuilding:* Students will deepen their
   design research and thinking methods, applying speculative design,
   worldbuilding, and MDA theory to create immersive, experiential futures.
   -

   *Apply Iterative Production Methodologies:* Students will practice
   digital production processes, emphasizing iterative testing, rapid
   prototyping, and agile methodologies, to develop an impactful final project.
   -

   *Collaborate with Industry Leaders:* Students will engage with leading
   practitioners from film, TV, theatre, gaming, and emergent technology
   fields, enhancing their collaborative skills and industry knowledge through
   direct participation and collaboration.

*How the course works*
1. Students work as a team
2. An RFP (request for proposals) is presented to the team
3. The RFP details a theme, location and time of a live event that will be
staged at the end of the semester.
4. The class works together and with mentor/guest speakers to design, build
and deploy a digital storytelling experience

*Mentors/Guest Speakers*The course has mentors/guest speakers who are
accessible to the students throughout the semester who cover the following
subject areas…

Experience Design
Creative technology
AI and Machine Learning
UX strategy & design
Strategic Foresight
Data Science

   - All skill levels are welcome and the course is open to students across
   the university. You do not need to take DS I or II prior. Instead all DS
   courses are designed to be taken in any order.
   - A full syllabus is available below - please note that it is subject to
   change.

*The course provides  *
- An introduction to immersive storytelling through a group project that
will live beyond the classroom
- An opportunity to collaborate with experts working at th

emails.subject: Permeable Artifact copy editing
emails.body:
I think we are now transitioning from adding new content to consolidating
and copy editing. We may not need until the 13th: if it is stable before
then we will submit.

Jocelyn provided suggestions on the institutional press section which I
tried to answer. This may take some more back and forth. Otherwise I think
we have the content we need. We just might be able to say it in a cleaner
or more poetic way.

We also have to write a reply to reviewers. We have a draft file in the
Google drive with that name. Once the manuscript is stable we will  go back
and resynchronize to make sure we didn't edit out what we promised to do.
Shiyan and I will spare you this task, but if you are interested or want to
help please see the reply to reviewers.

If you want to tell us when you want to copy edit, please do. Otherwise, we
can proceed in parallel. We should at this point put tracking on (suggested
editing) for everything but format changes, just in case an edit cuts into
something we know we need for a particular reviewer; Shiyan and I will try
to accept changes rapidly, as we will also be conscious of the paper
length.

If at some stage you are pretty much happy with the manuscript, send a note
like "Fine with current manuscript" or something like that. Once everyone
is OK Shiyan and I will do the final formatting and preparation for
submission.

If things go well this reviewing cycle, we will get another set of reviews,
and we will do another set of changes. They told us they will give us much
less revision time in the next round, if we get through this gauntlet.
Which is good: it implies they also are starting to converge.

------------------------------------------------------------

emails.subject: Fwd: Save the Date - Sept. 12th: Sports AI Innovation Symposium @ Columbia
emails.body:
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Eddie Mandhry <em3986@columbia.edu>
Date: Mon, Aug 4, 2025 at 11:49 AM
Subject: Save the Date - Sept. 12th: Sports AI Innovation Symposium @
Columbia
To: Eddie Mandhry <em3986@columbia.edu>

Greetings,
I hope this finds you well. I’m writing with a quick note to kindly ask you
to *save the date* for the *2025 Columbia–Dream Sports AI Innovation
Symposium*, which will take place on *Friday, September 12, from 9:00 AM to
4:00 PM* at *Pulitzer Hall, Columbia University*.

This year’s program will feature *keynote talks*, *industry fireside chats*,
and a select number of *lightning talks *showcasing research supported by
the Center.

*Ask:*
If you’re available to attend, we’d love to have you, or members of your
team join us for all or part of the convening. And if you’re potentially
interested in presenting your project during one of the lightning talk
sessions, please let me know. We’re still finalizing the program and
gauging presenter availability, so it would be helpful to know if you’d be
open to being included in a lighting round talk.

You’ll also receive a calendar invite shortly to help block the time.

More details will follow soon. Thanks again for being part of this growing
community.

Warmly,
Eddie
__
Eddie Mandhry
Managing Director
Columbia Dream Sports AI Innovation Center

>> *From: *Jeff Nickerson <jvnickerson@gmail.com>
>> *Date: *Tuesday, August 26, 2025 at 3:13 PM
>> *To: *Lydia Chilton <chilton@cs.columbia.edu>, Jocelyn McKinnon-Crowley <
>> jmckin02@syr.edu>, Zhang, Shiyan <shiyan.zhang@lancaster.ac.uk>, Karthik
>> Sreedhar <ks4190@columbia.edu>, Alice Cai <caia@mit.edu>
>> *Subject: *Evaluation for the paper
>>
>> We only have a couple of weeks before the deadline, and so only certain
>> things are possible. Still, it might make sense to think about how we can
>> have more not less evaluation.
>>
>> Since the story is about action design research, the more we can focus on
>> one case the better. But the podcast case so far only involves one person.
>> Here is my sense of things maybe we can do.
>>
>> 1. We are looking to do one more session with the Executive Producer.
>> Lydia, has he gotten back to you? I see him socially Saturday, so let me
>> know if we have something scheduled or what the back and forth has been.
>> 1b. We might want to ask him if we can share the prototype with one
>> of his writers. If we did something with a writer, that might give us
>> another organizational perspective.
>>
>> 2. We have the notes on the one session with the Managing Editor. Could
>> we do another one with him before the deadline? And/or someone underneath
>> the managing editor?
>>
>> This is not our primary case, but we could call it a parallel case, and
>> it raises print vs. broadcast journalism contexts.
>>
>> 3. Could we send out links to the prototype to a bunch of
>> journalists/journalism students along with an open-ended set of questions?
>>
>> I am not sure what we would ask, but I think we would like examples of
>> if/how the prototype helped find something new.
>>
>> 4. Are there some a/b tests or analyses we could do?
>> For example, turn on/off features at certain points in the interaction?
>> Compare to just chatgpt?
>> plot the conversations the participants have?
>>
>> Could we look to see what kind of personae get created and how?
>>
>> Along these lines, could we have stories with holes and see how long it
>> takes for them to get plugged in different configurations of the prototype?
>>
>> 5. Thinking out loud...
>> Anybody teaching journalists this semester? Could we get use data from an
>> assignment or an in class demo?
>>
>>
>>
>>

emails.subject: ​Subject: Beware of Email and Social Engineering Text Scams
emails.body:
[image: image.png]

Subject: Beware of Email and Social Engineering Text Scams

Dear Students, Faculty, and Staff,

With the start of the Fall term, Cybersecurity and Email Systems are
experiencing a surge of sophisticated phishing and social engineering
attempts. They are targeting our community with fake job offers of remote
internships and quick cash. These messages may look legitimate, but are
designed to steal money, personal information, and University credentials.

Current threat example:

   -

   Scammers are impersonating Columbia professors and offering fake
   Internships via email. After initial email communication, they move to
   text messaging and conduct fake interviews to gain trust. Next, they
   request personal information to set you up in the Columbia payroll system,
   followed by sending a legitimate looking $2500 check to deposit for office
   supplies. By the time your bank processes it, and realizes it's fake, it’s
   too late, and your provided information is compromised.

Common scams aimed at college students

   -

   Fake scholarship / financial-aid offers
   -

      Scammer emails or texts: “You’ve been awarded a scholarship/grant —
      just pay a small processing fee or click here to claim.”
      <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://financialwellness.utah.edu/blog/posts/2024/scams.php?utm_source=chatgpt.com__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WR3NSy3m5sYvYizBK1dAiPvVHYj2A8dguDbpChV95CIK6ul_KZu_vrFGpoMT9SGeHYWHefAvLpQylf5w7JYYPfQ0XNh9ozhMMg$ >
      -

      Red flags: unsolicited, “guaranteed” award, asks for payment or
      bank/SSN info to “release funds”.
      <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.needhambank.com/resources/a-guide-for-college-students-be-aware-of-these-4-scams/?utm_source=chatgpt.com__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WR3NSy3m5sYvYizBK1dAiPvVHYj2A8dguDbpChV95CIK6ul_KZu_vrFGpoMT9SGeHYWHefAvLpQylf5w7JYYPfQ0XNgKUPJmqA$ >Why
      students are targeted: they often seek financial help, and may be less
      risk-averse.

      -

   Fake job / remote work / “easy money” offers
   -

      Example: “Work from home, earn $2,000/week, just do data entry for
      us.”
      -

      Often involves sending/receiving checks, or providing bank details,
      or clicking links that lead to credential phishing.
      -

      Students doing part-time jobs or looking for flexibility are
      especially vulnerable.

      -

   Phishing emails targeting student accounts/university systems
   -

      Emails that appear to be from the university IT department: “Your
      student account will be suspended unless you verify your
credentials here.”
      -

      Links lead to fake login pages, credentials stolen, and campus
      systems compromised.
      -

   Fake apartment/moving/textbook deals
   -

      Scammers post appealing housing listings (“cheap, near campus”),
      request a deposit or personal info up front.
      -

      Fake textbook websites or sellers: you pay, but don’t receive the
      book (or give your payment/credit-card info).
      -

   Social-media / influencer/modeling “offers”
   -

      On Instagram/TikTok: “We want you to be our model/influencer. Send
      your banking info for it, or deposit a check, then transfer part back.”

      <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.uwcu.org/loans/articles/5-financial-scams-college-students?utm_source=chatgpt.com__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WR3NSy3m5sYvYizBK1dAiPvVHYj2A8dguDbpChV95CIK6ul_KZu_vrFGpoMT9SGeHYWHefAvLpQylf5w7JYYPfQ0XNgbGWv7cg$ >
      -

      Young adults are big consumers of social media, making them targets
      for these more “glamorous” scams.

      -

   Debt-relief / credit-repair lies
   -

      Students may already have loans, credit cards; scammers promise to
      “erase” debt or improve credit for a fee.

      -

   Fake or urgent billing/tuition notices
   -

      Email or text claiming the student owes tuition or fees, forcing them
      to click a link or pay immediately.

   -

   Smishing (SMS) or WhatsApp hook

emails.subject: Fw: Important Notice: XRF Testing at 110-114 Ridge Street
emails.body:
----- Forwarded Message ----- From: MD2 Support <support@md2pg.com>Cc: Al Shehada <alshehada@md2pg.com>; Amar Omeragic <amar.omeragic@md2pg.com>; Jacob Benezra <jacob.benezra@md2pg.com>Sent: Wednesday, August 6, 2025 at 01:29:54 PM PDTSubject: Important Notice: XRF Testing at 110-114 Ridge Street
 Dear Residents of 110-114 Ridge Street,

To comply with New York Law, we need to conduct a full inspection in each unit. We have hired Safety Universal LLC, a third-party company licensed by the EPA, to perform this testing.

A certified technician from Safety Universal will enter your apartment to conduct the testing. This process is professional, quick, and should take approximately 35 minutes. The technician will test all painted surfaces, and no preparation is needed from you.

For your safety, the technicians wear badges that you may request to see before they enter your unit. All CDC and health department regulations are followed at all times.

Safety Universal will contact you shortly to schedule your appointment. You can also reach them at (718) 676-8090 or office@safetyuniversalny.com.

Please ensure that access to your unit is granted for the scheduled appointment.

Feel free to reach out if you have any questions.

Sincerely,
MD Squared Property Group

Good Morning Karthik & Ashwin,

This email confirms that we have received notice that you will not be renewing your lease which expires on
June 30, 2025.

We hope you enjoyed your residency at Silver Towers!

The next step is to contact the Property Management Office at (212) 967-1330 or mailto:silvertowers@silvprop.com  to schedule the service elevator for your move out. Property Management will walk you through move out logistics and let you know the COI requirements.
 You may drop off your keys at the Property Management Office or the front desk in your lobby.

Please note that you must vacate the apartment and return keys on or before the lease expiration date. Please make sure that the apartment has been cleared of all belongings and is returned to us in broom clean condition.

In regards to your security deposit, it will be mailed to you by check within 14 business days of your lease expiration. Please leave a forwarding address with Property Management or you can send that information directly to our Tenant Billing team at mailto:tenantbilling@silvprop.com .
 After you submit payment for your last month of rent, please disable AutoPayment if you have it set up. If you have any questions regarding Autopayment or the return of your security deposit, please reach out to our Tenant Billing team at mailto:tenantbilling@silvprop.com 
 and 212-313-4634.

We would like to thank you for leasing with us. We hope you keep us in mind for a future rental and refer Silver Towers to others.

Best Regards,

Katelynn

Silverstein Properties, LLC

emails.subject: Re: 155 Ridge Street #4H StreetEasy Inquiry From Karthik Sreedhar
emails.body:
Hey Karthik! CONGRATS!

You are approved for 155 Ridge Street priced at $2950 starting on June 15th.

The lease will be sent to both of your emails from Stellar Management via DocuSign today (you might’ve already received it!).  It is easy to click through to sign.  There are a couple of options to pay the first month's rent and security...

OPTION ONE:  Make TWO SEPARATE Certified Bank Checks (aka cashier's checks) as follows...

* $2950 to "Stellar Management" (1st month rent)

* $2950 to "Stellar Management" (Security Deposit)

OPTION TWO -- ELECTRONIC OPTION FOR PAYMENTS:

At the end of the signing process, you will see that there is an option to pay rent/security by bank debit or credit card, with a service charge; or by ACH eCheck for free.  This is quick and convenient if you choose to pay by this method.  The payment link will be generated after you both complete the signing process.

Unfortunately our brokerage doesn’t take payment by card yet, so that would either need to be paid via bank wire or certified bank check (made out to “Beekman Street Realty “). Below is the information needed for the bank wire.

Wiring information...

Entity Name:  Beekman Street Realty LLC

Bank Name:  Capital One Bank

Account Number:  9054037255

Routing Number:  021407912

If the bank address is needed...

Capital One Bank

175 West 72nd Street

New York NY 10023

Fee amount…

$2950

If the office address is needed…

50 W 93rd Street

Suite 1N

New York NY 10025

I attached the welcome letter here.  It has important staff info.  , your super, will be the most important contact.  He lives at the property, and he essentially handles everything.  He'll give you keys etc.

Edin Kolenovic
Beekman Street Realty LLC
50 West 93rd Street Suite 1N

emails.subject: Clery Crime Alert - Burglaries / Package Thefts
emails.body:
[cid:image001.png@01DC523B.40BB0A20]

Clery Crime Alert

Burglary / Package Thefts

The Department of Public Safety was just informed that on Sunday, November 2, 2025, at approximately 4:30 p.m. a Burglary occurred at 414 West 120 Street. The same individual may have committed a similar crime on Saturday, November 8, 2025, at 5:18 p.m. at the same location. The individual is male presenting with a dark complexion and a beard. They were wearing a green hooded jacket, black jeans and red sneakers.

A video review shows the individual randomly pressing the intercom in an attempt to gain entry. Once inside of the building, the individual opened one package and removed the contents. The individual was last seen walking northbound on Amsterdam Ave.

If you have any additional information about this incident, please contact Public Safety at 212 854-2797.

Harm Reduction Strategies:

  *   Do not buzz people into a building unless you know who they are and what they are doing.
  *   Please try to retrieve your package in a timely manner.
  *
When safe and if possible, do not allow others to piggyback behind you when you are entering your apartment building. If you do not feel safe stopping a person from piggybacking behind you, you can always call Public Safety (212-854-2797) once you are in a safe location.
  *
Make sure the doors lock behind you every time to enter or exit a building.
  *
Stay informed about delivery statuses and estimated arrival times and try to retrieve your package in a timely manner.
  *
Many mail carriers (UPS, Fed Ex) have offices where you can drop-off and pick-up your packages safely. You can also arrange delivery to partnered local businesses, like pharmacies.

Disclaimer: Clery Crime Alerts (Timely Warning Notifications) are distributed pursuant to the Jeanne Clery Disclosure of Campus Security Policy and Campus Crime Statistics Act. Columbia University is required to alert the campus community when certain crimes occur in our Clery Act defined campuses that represent a serious or continuous threat to the community.

To see this and other Clery Crime Alerts please visit our Public Safety website<https://publicsafety.columbia.edu/clery-act-about  >. For more information about Columbia University’s Clery Act compliance efforts, please contact the Department’s Compliance Unit by emailing ps-compliance@columbia.edu<mailto:%20ps-compliance@columbia.edu>.

emails.subject: Clery Crime Alert: Burglaries - Package Thefts
emails.body:
[image.png]Clery Crime Alert
Burglaries - Package Thefts
Columbia University Public Safety was recently informed that on Thursday, November 6, 2025, between 3:29 p.m. and 3:43 p.m. three Burglaries occurred at 540 West 122nd Street, 520 West 122nd Street, and 506 West 122nd Street.
All incidents appeared to have been committed by the same suspect, a male-presenting person, with a slim build, dark complexion, dark mustache and beard, wearing a dark green jacket with a yellow hoodie underneath, and black pants, carrying an Aldi tote bag. They entered 540 West 122nd Street by ringing several apartments and then getting buzzed in by an unknown tenant. The same suspect also entered the 520 and 506 buildings by the same means, removing several packages from the main lobby.
No other information is available currently. If you have any additional information about this incident, please contact the Department of Public Safety at 212-854-2797.
Harm Reduction Strategies

  *
       When safe and if possible, do not allow others to piggyback behind you when you are entering your apartment building. If you do not feel safe stopping a person from piggybacking behind you, you can always call Public Safety (212-854-2797) once you are in a safe location.
  *
       Do NOT buzz people into your building if you do not know who they are.
  *
       Make sure the doors lock behind you every time to enter or exit a building.
  *
       Stay informed about delivery statuses and estimated arrival times and try to retrieve your package in a timely manner.
  *
       Many mail carriers (UPS, Fed Ex) have offices where you can drop-off and pick-up your packages safely. You can also arrange delivery to partnered local businesses, like pharmacies.

DISCLAIMER: Clery Crime Alerts (Timely Warning Notifications) are distributed pursuant to the Jeanne Clery Disclosure of Campus Security Policy and Campus Crime Statistics Act. Columbia University is required to alert the campus community when certain crimes occur in our Clery Act defined campuses that represent a serious or continuous threat to the community.
To see this and other Clery Crime Alerts please visit our Public Safety website<https://publicsafety.columbia.edu/content/clery-crime-alerts >. For more information about Columbia University’s Clery Act compliance efforts, please contact the Department’s Clery Act Compliance Director, Roberto A. Pineda Sanchez at ps-compliance@columbia.edu<mailto:ps-compliance@columbia.edu>.

Burglaries
Columbia University Public Safety was recently informed that on Sunday, November 2, 2025, between 3:43 p.m. and 3:45 p.m. two Burglaries occurred at 195 Claremont Avenue and 549 Riverside Drive.
Both incidents appeared to have been committed by the same suspect, a male-presenting person, with a slim build, dark complexion, gray beard, wearing dark framed glasses, a dark jacket, a black beanie with NY in white lettering, and dark jeans, carrying a Fresh Direct NY Yankees tote bag. They entered 195 Claremont Avenue by piggybacking behind tenants entering the building. Once inside, they were able to access 549 Riverside Drive. emails.subject: This Week at Columbia: Events & Opportunities
emails.body:
[View in your browser](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/view?reset=1&id=559&cid=6&cs=72fcb938a7d2bebe3a7c58cf4e7d3a95_1762280874_168  )

[University Life website](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=13971&qid=4507  )

Dear Students,

We hope you can take advantage of some of the opportunities below and enjoy a few moments of relaxation and connection.

Community Citizenship Initiative

The events below are designed to help students fulfill their [Community Citizenship Initiative](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=13972&qid=4507  ) (CCI) participation requirements. CCI is required for all newly enrolled students and optional for others.

http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=13972&qid=4507  

Peace & Presence: Coping Skills for Safety & Connection

Thursday, November 612:00 - 1:30 p.m.
Online

Explore how our autonomic nervous system reacts to stressors and trauma with [Counseling and Psychological Services](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=13973&qid=4507  ).

[Register for the Peace & Presence workshop](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=13974&qid=4507  )

Managing Conflict: Leading with Courage and Care (Session One)

Monday, November 10
12:00 - 1:30 p.m.
In-Person

Learn practical tools and strategies to approach challenging conversations thoughtfully, manage disagreements constructively, and lead with respect and cultural awareness.

[Register for the Managing Conflict workshop](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=13975&qid=4507  )

Managing Conflict: Leading with Courage and Care (Session Two)

Monday, November 10
3:00 - 4:30 p.m.
In-Person
Learn practical tools and strategies to approach challenging conversations thoughtfully, manage disagreements constructively, and lead with respect and cultural awareness.

[Register for the Managing Conflict workshop](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=13976&qid=4507  )

Orientalism, Enlightenment, and Human Endarkenment

Wednesday, November 12
2:30 - 4:00 p.m.In-Person

Join us for a lecture exploring how Orientalism is not just prejudice of West against East but that it functions at a deep structural level and stems from an epistemology that constituted the Enlightenment itself. It argues that in all our systems of modern life, Orientalism is philosophically and materially anti-human.

[Register for Orientalism, Enlightenment, and Human Endarkenment](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=13977&qid=4507  )

Courageous Conversations

Thursday, November 13
1:00 - 3:00 p.m.In-Person

Join this workshop developed by [LeaderShape, Inc](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=13978&qid=4507  ), for a session designed to help participants engage in honest, respectful, and authentic conversations across differences.

[Register for Courageous Conversations workshop](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=13979&qid=4507  )

Leading and Engaging in Society – Creating Positive Change (Session One)

Thursday, November 20
3:00 - 4:30 p.m.
In-Person

Reflect on your impact as a global citizen and an agent of positive social change in this workshop geared to help you strengthen your skills in empathy, collaboration, and community engagement.

[Register for the Leading and Engaging workshop](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=13980&qid=4507  )

University Life Events & Opportunities

All events listed below are in Eastern Time (ET), free, and open to all students across all schools and campuses.

http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=13981&qid=4507  

Drop-In Hours: Earl Hall

Today, November 4 | 3:00 - 6:00 p.m.
Hybrid

Stop by [Earl Hall](http://co

emails.subject: CU@SEAS — Engineering Student Affairs Newsletter 10.29.25
emails.body:
*Weekly News*
Office of Engineering Student Affairs | 10.29.25
[image: Description of the GIF]

*Sidewalk Repair Work in front of Mudd*
CU Facilities is planning to perform sidewalk repairs on 120th Street, from
the mid-block behind CEPSR to Amsterdam Ave in front of Mudd from *Saturday,
Nov 1 to Tuesday, Nov 4*. During those days, there will be jack-hammering
along the route from 9AM - 4PM daily.

*Beware of Email and Text Scams!*
CUIT has noted a recent uptick in sophisticated phishing and social
engineering attempts. These may look legitimate, but are designed to steal
money, personal information, and University credentials!

*Common scams include:*
Fake scholarship or
financial aid offers Fake job, remote work, or "easy money" offers Phishing
emails targeting student accounts
Fake apartment, moving,
or textbook deals Fake or urgent billing or tuition notices Debt-relief or
credit-repair lies

*Protect Yourself!*

   - *Do not share personal information* (SSN, bank details, IDs)
   - *Verify requests* using known contact information
   - *Be cautious* with links or attachments
   - *Check the sender*'s address and reply-to
   - *Treat urgent or secret payment requests as suspicious*

If you receive a suspicious message, do not reply and forward the message
to phishing@columbia.edu or contact CUIT at 212-854-1919.

emails.snippet: Spam and Low Priority Email Digest: 31 New Messages For Karthik Sreedhar ks4190@columbia.edu The messages in your personal Digest represent emails that have been filtered and sorted into different

emails.subject: Fw: Clery Crime Alert - Burglaries at 549 Riverside Drive and 195 Claremont Avenue
emails.body:
[cid:b59ec895-5b9a-4a9e-bf98-9234ffe365fa]
Clery Crime Alert
Burglaries

Columbia University Public Safety was informed of three burglaries that may have been committed by the same person. The first two burglaries occurred at 549 Riverside Drive: one on Monday, September 22 at around 5:00 p.m. and the other on Saturday, September 27, at around 4:59 p.m. The third burglary occurred on Saturday, September 27, at around 5:00 p.m. at 195 Claremont Avenue which is adjoined to 549 Riverside Drive. On each occasion, the suspect entered the building by piggybacking behind residents.

If you have any additional information about this incident, please contact the Department of Public Safety at 212-854-2797.

Description of Reported Offender:
The suspect was observed on CCTV cameras, as male-presenting, with a dark complexion, and a thin build. On September 22 they wore a gray coat, a black tee shirt with white graphics, a black, gray and white camouflage pair of pants and white sneakers. On September 27 they wore a white tee shirt, gray pants and black sneakers. No other information is available at this time.

Harm Reduction Strategies:

  *
When safe and if possible, do not allow others to piggyback behind you when you are entering your apartment building. If you do not feel safe stopping a person from piggybacking behind you, you can always call Public Safety (212-854-2797) once you are in a safe location.
  *
Many mail carriers (UPS, Fed Ex) have offices where you can drop-off and pick-up your packages safely. You can also arrange delivery to partnered local businesses, like pharmacies.
  *
When possible, arrange for packages to be delivered when you or someone else is available to receive them.
  *
Make sure the doors lock behind you every time to enter or exit a building.
  *
Stay informed about delivery statuses and estimated arrival times and try to retrieve your package in a timely manner.

DISCLAIMER: Clery Crime Alerts (Timely Warning Notifications) are distributed pursuant to the Jeanne Clery Disclosure of Campus Security Policy and Campus Crime Statistics Act. Columbia University is required to alert the campus community when certain crimes occur in our Clery Act defined campuses that represent a serious or continuous threat to the community.

To see this and other Clery Crime Alerts please visit our Public Safety website<https://publicsafety.columbia.edu/content/clery-crime-alerts  >. For more information about Columbia University’s Clery Act compliance efforts, please contact the Department’s Clery Act Compliance Director, Roberto A. Pineda Sanchez at ps-compliance@columbia.edu.

Office of Engineering Student Affairs | 09.10.25
[image: Description of the GIF]

*Fall 2025 Columbia Transportation Resources
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__columbiaengineering.campusgroups.com_click-3Femail-5Flinks-5Fid-3D386-26member-5Fid-3D11653489&d=DwMFAg&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=HMohsCmvn3khR59OZP8W1uAX7b1ZPtLqGHtGVQ9NDAU&m=ziHEmKMNkx6Om_SGFvsGLCDCRdVyJHCPUK9oKQFhAIH-P3PwXkKSUjWIvhsfKyGD&s=hs_NytiLGy3MkG6L9k3FcYwbbgAAhRa5ab7ImYZfn5w&e=>*
*Columbia Transportation*

Columbia operates a variety of shuttle buses between campuses. Shuttles
are free for CUID holders; just tap your CUID on the card reader as you
enter the bus!

Visit Columbia Transportation
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__columbiaengineering.campusgroups.com_click-3Femail-5Flinks-5Fid-3D387-26member-5Fid-3D11653489&d=DwMFAg&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=HMohsCmvn3khR59OZP8W1uAX7b1ZPtLqGHtGVQ9NDAU&m=ziHEmKMNkx6Om_SGFvsGLCDCRdVyJHCPUK9oKQFhAIH-P3PwXkKSUjWIvhsfKyGD&s=-tq-XpQMOQiSYEVLUzCa5PqQ_0GPJJ9-0ZqOs2Qd9j8&e=>

the new bus tracker app TripShot.

until 3 AM within a coverage area surrounding the Morningside and
Manhattanville campuses. Find more information on the Columbia
Transportation site here
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__columbiaengineering.campusgroups.com_click-3Femail-5Flinks-5Fid-3D388-26member-5Fid-3D11653489&d=DwMFAg&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=HMohsCmvn3khR59OZP8W1uAX7b1ZPtLqGHtGVQ9NDAU&m=ziHEmKMNkx6Om_SGFvsGLCDCRdVyJHCPUK9oKQFhAIH-P3PwXkKSUjWIvhsfKyGD&s=vXz2PDlWp7syEpHfI3gmIyaqAJzEtaECzM24qWF57yQ&e=>

in one place!

emails.body:
*Weekly News*
Office of Engineering Student Affairs | 09.03.25
[image: Description of the GIF]
*Welcome back, Engineers!*

Happy Fall 2025 and welcome back to campus! We hope you're feeling ready to
dive into a semester filled with new opportunities, challenges, and growth.
The Graduate Student Life team is excited to bring you an array of events
designed to help you connect, unwind, and make the most of your time here
at Columbia.

*We're looking forward to seeing you around this semester!*

*Fall 2025 Columbia Transportation Resources
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__columbiaengineering.campusgroups.com_click-3Femail-5Flinks-5Fid-3D316-26member-5Fid-3D11653489&d=DwMGaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=HMohsCmvn3khR59OZP8W1uAX7b1ZPtLqGHtGVQ9NDAU&m=l2CS9SxQSLuTbLGl7aJJokrLYw27qjC1Q1ZWiQua6QO6MXGbUsQvDdWFdT6uy-_X&s=nLkc0GnNlbIz3VrZFQuDKQSyOTVYnGuGpnpzjIWwuww&e=>*
*Columbia Transportation*

Columbia operates a variety of shuttle buses between campuses. Shuttles
are free for CUID holders; just tap your CUID on the card reader as you
enter the bus!

Visit Columbia Transportation
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__columbiaengineering.campusgroups.com_click-3Femail-5Flinks-5Fid-3D317-26member-5Fid-3D11653489&d=DwMGaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=HMohsCmvn3khR59OZP8W1uAX7b1ZPtLqGHtGVQ9NDAU&m=l2CS9SxQSLuTbLGl7aJJokrLYw27qjC1Q1ZWiQua6QO6MXGbUsQvDdWFdT6uy-_X&s=_25Hr1V2QP6uSu60sVJ6X4L4DAT2thgu7mgiC9E9_JI&e=>
for updated information on Fall 2025 schedules, updated stop locations, and
the new bus tracker app TripShot.

*Evening Shuttle Service*
Columbia also operates an on-demand Evening Shuttle service each evening
until 3 AM within a coverage area surrounding the Morningside and
Manhattanville campuses. Find more information on the Columbia
Transportation site here
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__columbiaengineering.campusgroups.com_click-3Femail-5Flinks-5Fid-3D318-26member-5Fid-3D11653489&d=DwMGaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=HMohsCmvn3khR59OZP8W1uAX7b1ZPtLqGHtGVQ9NDAU&m=l2CS9SxQSLuTbLGl7aJJokrLYw27qjC1Q1ZWiQua6QO6MXGbUsQvDdWFdT6uy-_X&s=LW3wM4BiXLcnyBlf5u-wYa299UOTE98mLEx1PloWuzU&e=>
!

*Mood Board Monday *
Set the tone. Set your goals. Set yourself up for success. Join us to
create a mood board or vision board for this upcoming semester.
*No registration required.*
Monday, Sep 8 | 3 - 4:30 PM
Back of Carleton Commons

*Board at Noon*
Not bored -- just boardgames! Join us to take a small break in the middle
of the week to play card and board games. Bring your own or play one of the
games we'll have available.
*No registration required.*
Wednesday, Sep 10 | 12 - 2:30 PM
Back of Carleton Commons

*Apartment Warming with Pot Painting*
Join us to paint pots and collect succulents to add some fresh greenery to
your space!
**Available while supplies last*
*No registration required.*
Thursday, Sep 18 | 11 AM
Back of Carleton Commons
Reminder that the *Engineering Graduate Student Council (EGSC)*
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__columbiaengineering.campusgroups.com_click-3Femail-5Flinks-5Fid-3D319-26member-5Fid-3D11653489&d=DwMGaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=HMohsCmvn3khR59OZP8W1uAX7b1ZPtLqGHtGVQ9NDAU&m=l2CS9SxQSLuTbLGl7aJJokrLYw27qjC1Q1ZWiQua6QO6MXGbUsQvDdWFdT6uy-_X&s=4s3mYOe12jMesqxCKIA3V12kUWfYg1gJMgirWQmpt50&e=>is
here to serve students in discussing their concerns, foster community and
respect amongst students through events, and so much more!

The 2025 E-Board would like to welcome you all back to Columbia, and looks
forward to seeing you all this coming semester 💙

emails.subject: Clery Crime Alert: Burglaries
emails.body:
[image.png]

The Department of Public Safety has been informed of a burglary that occurred on September 1, 2025, at 195 Claremont Avenue and 549 Riverside Drive.

A video review shows a suspect piggybacking behind a tenant to gain access into 195 Claremont Avenue on September 1, 2025 at 1:30am. Once inside the building, the suspect proceeds to take two packages from the lobby. Afterwards, the suspect proceeds through a passageway into the lobby of 549 Riverside Drive where they take four additional packages.

The individual is described as male-presenting, with a medium complexion, and facial hair. If you have any additional information, please contact Public Safety at 212-854-2797.

Safety Tips

  *   Stay informed about delivery statuses and estimated arrival times.
  *   Please try to retrieve your package in a timely manner.
  *   Request that packages be placed in a less visible or concealed spot.
  *   When possible, arrange for packages to be delivered when you or someone else is available to receive them.
  *   When safe and if possible, do not allow others to piggyback behind you when you are entering your apartment building. If you do not feel safe stopping a person from piggybacking behind you, you can always call Public Safety (212-854-2797) once you are in a safe location.

Disclaimer: Clery Crime Alerts (Timely Warning Notifications) are distributed to the Jeanne Clery Disclosure of Campus Security Policy and Campus Crime Statistics Act. Columbia University is required to alert the campus community when certain crimes occur in our Clery Act defined campuses that represent a serious or continuous threat to the community.

To see this and other Clery Crime Alerts, please visit our Public Safety website<https://publicsafety.columbia.edu/content/clery-crime-alerts  >. For more information about Columbia University’s Clery Act compliance efforts, please contact the Department’s Clery Act Compliance Director, Roberto A. Pineda Sanchez at ps-compliance@columbia.edu.

Community News

In collaboration with partners in and outside of Columbia, we have a full
roster of events starting on Monday, September 22 with the first Tech CEO
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.eventbrite.com_e_tech-2Dceo-2Dlecture-2Dalexander-2Dsarrigeorgiou-2Deurolife-2Dffh-2Dinsurance-2Dgroup-2Dtickets-2D1478627838669-3Faff-3Doddtdtcreator&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=IldiK8XidMOdHX62LG8nFWqQiR7dDww4PcxZ6lZOm1k&m=UwMTzsPdzu9NsfGkOEEkMR0lEFmECNOVAMF24DN0a8lyE3W1C2On25u4HmUBrJXn&s=-2-tpOW-hvFJSA9Wi97oMonWH9ypymsRXrGjhEkuZZc&e=>
lecture of the year, co-hosted with Columbia Business School.

Please see the Climate Week website
<https://www.engineering.columbia.edu/about/events/climate-week-columbia-engineering-2025?utm_source=Columbia+Engineering+Communications&utm_campaign=dd3ffe2990-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_08_20_02_46_COPY_02&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-dc2af10a51-  >
for
the schedule and event locations.
Community Events

Columbia Engineering celebrates summer! On June 24, members of the
Engineering community celebrated Pride Month with an ice-cream social and
fun and games in Pupin Plaza.

On August 13, staff gathered again for the Summer Staff Social to
recognize employee milestones and enjoy food and games.
Community Events

Broadway Community Volunteer Event
<https://admin.engineering.columbia.edu/news/broadway-community-volunteer-event-engineering-humanity-action  >

Columbia Engineering staff volunteered in the neighborhood to support Broadway
Community
<https://www.communityservice.columbia.edu/content/broadway-community-inc-0  >,
a nonprofit organization dedicated to addressing food insecurity and
promoting human dignity. As part of the semester’s “Spring into Action”
theme, volunteers prepared and served food, engaged with guests, and helped
with cleanup.

Take a Break Meditation Series
<https://admin.engineering.columbia.edu/news/take-break-meditation-series-moment-stillness-busy-day  >

In April, Columbia Engineering staff participated in *Take a Break*, a
guided meditation series facilitated by Columbia’s ergonomist, Kevin Myers,
MS, AEP, ATC. The series offered simple techniques to help staff manage
stress and improve focus and overall well-being.

Community News
Teaming Up to Transform Pediatric Brain Cancer
<https://www.engineering.columbia.edu/about/news/teaming-transform-pediatric-brain-cancer-treatment  >

Robert and Margaret Hariri Professor of Biomedical Engineering Elisa
Konofagou and Stergios Zacharoulis, a pediatric oncologist at Columbia
University Irving Medical Center, are working together to pioneer a
groundbreaking approach that uses focused ultrasound to treat a type of
cancerous tumor in children and other brain diseases.
An AI-Enhanced Education
<https://www.engineering.columbia.edu/about/news/ai-enhanced-education  >

Faculty across the Engineering School and partners are finding new and
innovative ways to integrate generative AI in the classroom.
*New Dean of Undergraduate Student Life
<https://www.college.columbia.edu/news/college-engineering-announce-new-dean-undergraduate-student-life-kamala-kiem  >*

Meet Kamala C. Kiem, the new Dean of Undergraduate Student Life for
Columbia Engineering and Columbia College.

*Michael Weinstein Elected to the American Academy of Arts & Sciences
<https://www.apam.columbia.edu/weinstein-elected-american-academy-arts-and-sciences  >*

The professor of applied mathematics and of mathematics, an expert in
nonlinear waves and coherent structures, was recognized for his exceptiona

emails.subject: Fw: Scholarship Opportunities for Engineering Students
emails.body:
----- Forwarded Message ----- From: Jayanthi subramanian <jayanthi@stanford.edu>To: "students@cs.stanford.edu" <students@cs.stanford.edu>Sent: Monday, October 27, 2025 at 03:19:13 PM PDTSubject: Scholarship Opportunities for Engineering Students
    
I’m reaching out on behalf of the American Council of Engineering Companies (ACEC) Research Institute to share several scholarship opportunities available to qualified undergraduate and graduate engineering students for the 2026–2027 academic year.
 
 We would greatly appreciate your help in sharing this information with your students. By spreading the word, you can help ensure more aspiring engineers have access to financial support that can make a meaningful difference in their education and professional development.
 
 The application deadline for most scholarships is March 13, 2026.
 
 Please find attached a promotional graphic and suggested language you can include in your communications with students
    
 The ACEC Research Institute is the leading source of knowledge and thought leadership dedicated to creating a more sustainable, safe, secure, and technically advanced built environment. Through research, forecasts, trend analysis, and academic scholarships, the Institute provides insights and strategies that strengthen and elevate the engineering profession.
 
 Through the ACEC Research Institute’s online scholarship portal, qualified students can access more than 100 scholarships totaling nearly $1 million in awards. These include a $5,000 scholarship specifically designated for students from California, as well as merit and need-based opportunities.
 
 Students complete one application through the portal, which will automatically match them with all scholarships for which they qualify. Application deadlines vary, so students are encouraged to apply as early as possible.
 
 https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://acecinstitute.awardspring.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!TRP0F2n0_Reept3Q0xJjZwctt9bo9c4WwVSXMAtw7ajqobOE4PBkTRvxeg_yOIfRZ9hBlh7WYgAYA45ipNqOQHpgbke0HqmY$ 
    
 If you have any questions or need additional information about these scholarships or other Institute initiatives, please don’t hesitate to contact me. Thank you for helping us reach and support the next generation of engineering leaders!
 
 Mira Schueler
 
 Manager, Engagement
 
 ACEC Research Institute
 
 Office: 202-347-7474
 
 Mobile: 202-682-4304
 
 1400 L Street NW, Suite 400
 
 Washington, DC 20005-3529
 
 Email: mschueler@acec.org

emails.subject: Fw: Action Required: Rent Reminder
emails.body:
Reference to water/sewer fee too in this mail.
    ----- Forwarded Message ----- From: MD2 Support <support@md2pg.com>Cc: Al Shehada <alshehada@md2pg.com>; Amar Omeragic <amar.omeragic@md2pg.com>; Jacob Benezra <jacob.benezra@md2pg.com>Sent: Tuesday, September 2, 2025 at 11:14:42 AM PDTSubject: Action Required: Rent Reminder
 
Dear Tenants,

This is a general reminder to all tenants that rent is due on the 1st of each month. We kindly ask you to ensure that your rent payment is submitted on time. Timely payments are essential for maintaining the smooth operation of the building and ensuring all services and facilities continue to function effectively.

Please also remember to double-check that your Water/Sewer Fee is paid in full alongside your rent.
If payment is not received by the 5th of the month, a late fee will be applied to your account. To avoid this fee, we strongly encourage you to submit your payment today or tomorrow if you have not already done so.

Your prompt attention to this matter is greatly appreciated. Thank you for fulfilling this important responsibility.

Best Regards, 

MD Squared Property Group

emails.subject: Fw: Please complete your surgical questionnaire
emails.body:
----- Forwarded Message ----- From: UCSF | Dr. Metz' s Team <ucsf@patientoutcomes.com>To: "sreedhar_sivakumaran@yahoo.com" <sreedhar_sivakumaran@yahoo.com>Sent: Sunday, August 24, 2025 at 06:26:30 AM PDTSubject: Please complete your surgical questionnaire
   
Dear Karthik,
 
We want to remind you that you have not yet completed your 5-year postoperative assessment, which is a valuable tool for measuring the results of surgeries like yours. The window during which you can take this assessment is closing rapidly, and it is very important we receive it in the next few days in order for it to be used as an accurate measurement of your progress.
 
We encourage you to please click the link below and spend a few minutes answering these questions. While you can take it at any time of the day or night, we do ask you complete it as soon as possible so we can compare your results to what is expected in the 5-year window and measure whether your recovery is progressing as it should.
 
If you have undergone additional or revision surgeries, we ask you still complete this assessment, as it includes questions about other surgeries you may have had. You can use the comments section of these questions to give your feedback about all of your treatment.
 
Please click this link to begin:
 
https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__ucsf.patientoutcomes.com_s_eff14baf23859bbc&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=w6L6yTvbPGrEJiuLRyHp1lqueUX-M5-k53Zd7Lk0-RzY-XU4abv5AQEprjc6HFX2&s=6IxIA7-p-Svnwo17wI4ma7KOe2ATURqM9Zia8stQmXc&e= 
 
If you have any questions or concerns about your surgery or this assessment, please visit the assessment program's Frequently Asked Questions page.
 
Thank you for your time,
 
Dr. Lionel Metz, MD 
 Associate Professor, Spine Surgery 
 UCSF Health

Your participation in our patient outcome program is completely voluntary. If you do not wish to participate in this important program for monitoring your recovery, please reply to this email with a message "I do not wish to participate." Replies to this email address are read and responded to by the patient outcome team, not your doctor. Because email is not a secure way of transmitting confidential information, we ask you please refrain from including personally-identifying information, such as your date of birth or phone number, in your email replies (however, you can securely share this or other personal medical details in the assessment itself). For all scheduling, records, or medical questions and assistance, please contact your doctor’s office directly (using the contact info on the Frequently Asked Questions page).

emails.subject: Fw: Measuring your recovery (Post-Operative Questionnaire)
emails.body:
I recommend you complete the questionnaire explaining what has happened in about the one year since the last surgery. Right now, my worry is every 3-4 months, it gets aggravated and whether there is anything that they would suggest to mitigate. Thanks.
    ----- Forwarded Message ----- From: UCSF | Dr. Metz' s Team <ucsf@patientoutcomes.com>To: "sreedhar_sivakumaran@yahoo.com" <sreedhar_sivakumaran@yahoo.com>Sent: Thursday, August 14, 2025 at 06:55:08 AM PDTSubject: Measuring your recovery (Post-Operative Questionnaire)
   
Dear Karthik,
 
We want to remind you that we would like your input on several questions needed to measure the results of the surgery on your right spine. This assessment is time-sensitive, and it is very important we receive it in the next few days in order for it to be used as an accurate measurement of your progress.
 
We encourage you to please click the link below and spend 10-20 minutes answering these questions. While you can take it at any time of the day or night, we do ask you complete it as soon as possible so we can compare your results to what is expected in the 5-year window and measure whether your recovery is progressing as it should.
 
If you have undergone additional or revision surgeries, we ask you still complete this assessment, as it includes questions about other surgeries you may have had. You can use the comments section of these questions to give your feedback about all of your treatment.
 
Please click this link to begin:
 
https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__ucsf.patientoutcomes.com_s_eff14baf23859bbc&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=K85oXEpzTTQ-_oylM8Hyvg3GP-Aj83Gte4gpfcaRV5_3Q9ZYZPy_KU2XJ7OxoHn-&s=mRW-GoU4xXyI3H_u_uz2mbf0u0xpgqKc3Q_GYQU1SdA&e= 
 
If you have any questions or concerns about your surgery or this assessment, please visit the assessment program's Frequently Asked Questions page.
 
Thank you for your time,
 
Dr. Lionel Metz, MD 
 Associate Professor, Spine Surgery 
 UCSF Health

Your participation in our patient outcome program is completely voluntary. If you do not wish to participate in this important program for monitoring your recovery, please reply to this email with a message "I do not wish to participate." Replies to this email address are read and responded to by the patient outcome team, not your doctor. Because email is not a secure way of transmitting confidential information, we ask you please refrain from including personally-identifying information, such as your date of birth or phone number, in your email replies (however, you can securely share this or other personal medical details in the assessment itself). For all scheduling, records, or medical questions and assistance, please contact your doctor’s office directly (using the contact info on the Frequently Asked Questions page).

emails.subject: Re: Capital One payment
emails.body:
It is your card and your income but I genuinely feel it is a mistake to be charging dining with friends to your card. We have so far has a great credit history. Now what we are doing is creating large balances - well over 30% of credit limit - which is a big downside to good credit history. You raked up $517 in 4 days from this past Thu to today Sun, and the bigger expenses are dining which I assume is with a set of people and I have to assume they can pay for themselves. Just something to really keep in mind because you don't need to hurt your credit history with >30% balances paying for friends even if they repay you promptly.     On Tuesday, July 15, 2025 at 06:38:38 PM PDT, Sreedhar Sivakumaran <sreedhar_sivakumaran@yahoo.com> wrote:  
 
  The balance is now $2803.25 and credit score is down by 4. Your are still tracking to ~1350 to $1450 every two weeks (June 28 to July 15 is 2803.25-1456) which seems over budget. I forget when you said Columbia will pay you but you need to pay down current bill as soon as they pay you.

Beyond this, you should think about saving some money every couple of weeks. Flights to CA, potential moving charges next year or two, and any other such expenses. I can cover if needed, but you should start routine savings now that you have regular salary instead of all earnings turning to expenses each month.
     On Saturday, June 28, 2025 at 03:08:48 PM PDT, Sreedhar Sivakumaran <sreedhar_sivakumaran@yahoo.com> wrote:  
 
  Okay, let me know when deposited and what amount I should take. 

I assume you are watching the expenses. I paid on June 14. In the two weeks since then, June 28, the balance is 1185.99 with 269.95 pending which is about 1456 which is to say your monthly expense is tracking to 2912 (almost 3000) just on this card alone which I think is higher than what you budgeted.     On Saturday, June 14, 2025 at 04:12:32 PM PDT, Karthik Sreedhar <ks4190@columbia.edu> wrote:  
 
 No no some are Norway but not most, the first two paychecks I get from Columbia for the summer you should just take then for this payment and the one you did in Norway, I’ll tell you when they’re deposited (but should be early and mid July). 
Then those charges you listed are valid but are very unfortunate I was paying utilities etc. with my Venmo and accidentally had the payment method as my credit card instead of my account but fixed now.

On Jun 14, 2025, at 10:23 AM, Sreedhar Sivakumaran <sreedhar_sivakumaran@yahoo.com> wrote:

﻿I paid the Capital One balance for this month $1,485. 82 assuming a lot of it will be Norway charges on your card. emails.subject: Fw: Resident Memo – Community Guidelines & Building Security
emails.body:
I think you said you get these direct.
    ----- Forwarded Message ----- From: MD2 Support <support@md2pg.com>Cc: Al Shehada <alshehada@md2pg.com>; Amar Omeragic <amar.omeragic@md2pg.com>; Jacob Benezra <jacob.benezra@md2pg.com>Sent: Monday, July 7, 2025 at 12:12:31 PM PDTSubject: Resident Memo – Community Guidelines & Building Security
 Dear Residents,

Management is committed to maintaining a safe and comfortable living environment. Residents are urged to follow these important guidelines:

   - Be mindful of noise levels—please refrain from playing loud music or TV between 9:30 PM and 9:00 AM.
   - Clean up after yourself in all shared spaces, including hallways, staircases, and laundry rooms.
   - Dispose of garbage and recycling properly—please use designated bins and avoid leaving trash in hallways or common areas.
   - Smoking is strictly prohibited anywhere on the premises. Violations will result in a $250 fine.
   - Do not buzz in anyone you do not personally know. Unauthorized entry compromises the safety of the entire building.
   - Ensure the front door fully closes and locks behind you when entering or exiting the building.
   - If you notice someone inside the building who appears unauthorized, report it to management immediately.
   - Package safety: If you are expecting a delivery, retrieve it as soon as possible to help prevent theft. If your building has a designated package room, please ensure the door is securely closed after use.

We appreciate your cooperation in keeping our community safe and secure. If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to reach out.

Best Regards,

MD Squared Property Group
emails.snippet: I think you said you get these direct. ----- Forwarded Message ----- From: MD2 Support <support@ md2pg. com> Cc: Al Shehada <alshehada@ md2pg. com>; Amar Omeragic <amar. omeragic@ md2pg.

emails.subject: Fw: Important Reminder: Building Security, Package Safety, and Neighborly Etiquette
emails.body:
I am not sure why you are not directly copied.
    ----- Forwarded Message ----- From: MD2 Support <support@md2pg.com>Cc: Amar Omeragic <amar.omeragic@md2pg.com>; Al Shehada <alshehada@md2pg.com>; Jacob Benezra <jacob.benezra@md2pg.com>Sent: Wednesday, July 2, 2025 at 08:52:17 AM PDTSubject: Important Reminder: Building Security, Package Safety, and Neighborly Etiquette
 Dear residents at 110-114 Ridge Street, 

We hope this message finds you well! As part of our continued effort to maintain a safe, secure, and welcoming environment for everyone, we’d like to remind all residents of a few important guidelines:

Package Security
   
   - Ensure the package room is securely closed and locking properly after use.
   - Retrieve your packages as soon as possible to minimize the risk of theft.

Building Security
   
   - Do not buzz in individuals you do not know or allow access to anyone who isn’t your guest.
   - Always make sure building doors close and lock behind you when entering or exiting the property.
   - If you notice any issues with locks or doors, please report them to management immediately.
   - If you notice any suspicious individuals please contact management immediately. 

Being a Kind Neighbor
   
   - Smoking is not permitted anywhere inside the building, including hallways, stairwells, and common areas.
   - Please be mindful of noise levels and refrain from playing loud music or creating disruptive noise between 10:00 PM and 9:00 AM.
   - Help keep shared spaces clean by properly disposing of trash and recycling.
   - Please do not leave items or garbage in common areas. 

We appreciate your cooperation in making our building a safe and comfortable place for everyone to live. If you have any questions or concerns, please don’t hesitate to contact us.

Thank you for being a valued member of our community!

Best regards,
MD Squared Property Group

emails.subject: Fwd: [ee-students-forum] IMPORTANT: Commencement Planning Information, June 15, 2025
emails.body:
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Laura Wuethrich <lwuet72@stanford.edu>
Date: Mon, Mar 17, 2025 at 11:33 PM
Subject: [ee-students-forum] IMPORTANT: Commencement Planning Information,
June 15, 2025
To: ee-students-forum@lists.stanford.edu <
ee-students-forum@lists.stanford.edu>, ee-students@lists.stanford.edu <
ee-students@lists.stanford.edu>

Dear Students and Graduates,

*Commencement (graduation) will take place on Sunday, June 15, 2025*. Below
is the information you need to know if you want to participate or simply
attend as a guest.

   -  *Ordering a Cap and Gown/General Commencement Information*

   - Order your cap and gown at the  Stanford Bookstore (
      https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__commencement.stanford.edu_graduation-2Dgear-2Dstanford-2Dbookstore&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=DMQOYTUglFVMdix-YaiT1FHnvJF6dONdVAK8GoKzIiJxcGxEImj-6-1RPQLrnzqL&s=1_pPz1PTubZ74MIx0m5VVc1HCGvT5RtUZyqv9QNXnOA&e= 
      ) as soon as possible. (*Deadline: Friday, May 2*)
      - You must wear a cap and gown to participate in the ceremony.

   - *Visit **https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__commencement.stanford.edu_-2A&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=DMQOYTUglFVMdix-YaiT1FHnvJF6dONdVAK8GoKzIiJxcGxEImj-6-1RPQLrnzqL&s=z9lV7RpOhiW3HRijs7t-k3ElrGtjv1F7XdusYx-3UPQ&e= 
   <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__commencement.stanford.edu_&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=DMQOYTUglFVMdix-YaiT1FHnvJF6dONdVAK8GoKzIiJxcGxEImj-6-1RPQLrnzqL&s=Fkrh9tqMUeYAD_uz-I5TVGkCf4sKYzbrs2smA_aiYB4&e= >* now for* *Frequently Asked
   Questions* <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__commencement.stanford.edu_frequently-2Dasked-2Dquestions&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=DMQOYTUglFVMdix-YaiT1FHnvJF6dONdVAK8GoKzIiJxcGxEImj-6-1RPQLrnzqL&s=w0y06Uuyg7h6UlTzIBd6QIK6CgjMXwhDCminFrNEEW4&e= >*,
   etc.  *

   - Please direct your families and guests to this page. This site has
      information about all weekend activities. This includes the main
university
      ceremony for all departments, held at the Stanford Stadium on *Sunday,
      June 15, at 9:30am*. Afterward, graduates attend individual
      department ceremonies held throughout campus.
      - *Note:* Attendance at the Stanford Stadium ceremony is optional,
      but there are rules for admittance so be sure to visit the website if you
      plan to participate.

   - *EE Department Commencement Information* for you and your guests
   including the full agenda, parking information, OAE accommodation requests,
   etc.

   - *ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING COMMENCEMENT WEB SITE: *
      https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__ee.stanford.edu_academics_commencement&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=DMQOYTUglFVMdix-YaiT1FHnvJF6dONdVAK8GoKzIiJxcGxEImj-6-1RPQLrnzqL&s=HUuxDY9MJrrHq1-ND0ZsBZsjD7olE5jCsWaKgbPBHTQ&e= 

   - If you are conferring your degree AFTER June 2025 (Summer Quarter
         2024-2025 or later) and want to participate in this year’s graduation
         celebration, please visit *here
         <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__ee.stanford.edu_academics_commencement_applying-2Dto-2Dgraduate&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=DMQOYTUglFVMdix-YaiT1FHnvJF6dONdVAK8GoKzIiJxcGxEImj-6-1RPQLrnzqL&s=hkTwV8-rBUqJhc_kwePw9hL-6pcR-85QppNqufepr74&e= >*
         to review the requirements and complete an *EE Walkthrough
         Petition.*

   - The EE ceremony will be held on the Alumni Lawn. It is located between
      the David Packard Building and Li Ka Shing Center (LKSC). See
the location

| View in browser|nytimes.com

|  |
|  |

 |
|  |

 |

|  |
| November 7, 2025

 |
|  |
|  |

|  | 
By Sam Sifton
 |
|  |

Good morning. Hundreds of flights across the U.S. have been canceled today because of the government shutdown. Tesla shareholders yesterday approved the world’s first trillion-dollar pay package, for Elon Musk. (That’s a big number!)

And I have two stories to share from the world of health care. Trump announced a deal with drugmakers to lower the cost of anti-obesity drugs, and my colleagues have a story explaining why emergency C-sections may not always be necessary.

Let’s dive in.

|  |
|  |
|  |

|  |
| A plane lands in Los Angeles. Mario Tama/Getty Images |

Expect delays

This morning, the Transportation Department is reducing the number of flights at 40 airports across the country. About 4 percent of flights will be cut today, officials estimated. That could rise to 10 percent by next Friday. The transportation secretary, Sean Duffy, said on Wednesday that the reductions in flights were meant to “alleviate the pressure” on air traffic controllers, some of whom have taken second jobs because they aren’t being paid.

Yesterday, the department was silent about details of where the pain points will be. But travelers are going to feel them today. Administration officials told Times reporters yesterday to expect cuts across the country.
