emails.subject: Complete Your Netflix Software Engineering Internship Application | Next Steps
emails.body:
Hi Karthik,
 
Thank you for your interest in our Netflix Software Engineering Internship role!
 
In order for your application to be reviewed, please complete this Airtable form. Your application will only be considered complete after you submit this form in conjunction with the application you already submitted on our Netflix Careers site. Please only complete the form once; duplicate submissions will not be considered.
 
Due to the large volume of applications we receive, we're sorry that we're unable to personally follow up with each applicant. Our full recruiting process may take several weeks, so we kindly ask for your patience as we complete candidate reviews and interviews. In order to maintain a fair and equitable hiring process, we will not be able to accommodate very tight deadlines.
 
For applicants selected to proceed, the interview process will begin with a CodeSignal Assessment. The results from this assessment will be used as just one additional data point to determine who will be invited to the interview rounds. The first two rounds of interviews are technical/coding interviews with Netflix software engineers, and the final round consists of both technical and behavioral questions. Your advancement will be based on the feedback at each round.
 
Important Note: If you're selected to move forward, the next email you receive will come from CodeSignal; we strongly recommend adding no-reply@codesignal.com to your contacts to prevent spam filtering.
 
Our goal is to reach out to candidates who are chosen for initial next steps within the next two to three weeks. If you haven’t heard from us by then, we may have reached interview capacity.

Note that our Software Engineering intern interview process is designed to assess your generalist skills, coding fundamentals, and aptitude. Offers will be extended to join our 2025 Intern class at Netflix, where we’ll have 3 start date options in Summer 2025 to centralize onboarding programming and training.

Thanks again, and good luck!

emails.subject: Re: Columbia M.S. Student interested in your work
emails.body:
Hi Karthik,

I'd love to learn more about your work! I am not in the bay these days as I'm in Paris for an internship but would be happy to chat via Zoom. If you're still interested, let me know when you're available to meet.

All the best,
Julia
________________________________
From: Karthik Sreedhar <ks4190@columbia.edu>
Sent: Thursday, August 22, 2024 18:53
To: Julia Mae Markel <jmarkel@stanford.edu>
Subject: Columbia M.S. Student interested in your work

Hi Julia,

My name is Karthik and I am a master's student at Columbia in the computer science and journalism dual degree program applying to PhD programs this fall.

I came across your work and was extremely interested; I am visiting the bay area (week of August 26th) and wanted to ask if you'd be willing to briefly meet on campus to chat about your research, as well as your group at Stanford. I currently work on research using LLMs to simulate human behavior and aid in code synthesis, but used to contribute to education research while an undergrad at UC Berkeley, and hence would love to talk about your work which seemed to be in the intersection between the two.

I sincerely appreciate your time and look forward to hearing from you!

Thanks,
Karthik

Hi Karthik ,

Thank you for applying to Machine Learning & Artificial Intelligence Engineering Internship – Summer 2024 ( 3053324 ) and choosing Qualcomm as a prospective employer, where we are passionate about our people and the limitless potential of your career. Unfortunately, we decided to move forward with other applicants.

Although this internship did not work out, please keep an eye out for our 2025 summer intern positions to be posted this fall. If there is a new internship that's a good fit, don’t hesitate to apply! Of course, we'll keep your information on file, as you may be a good fit for future positions.

We appreciate your interest in Qualcomm and wish you the best of luck with your search.

Thank you,
Qualcomm Talent Acquisition Team

We are limitless.

emails.subject: NLP related internship opportunity: COMSW4705_002_2024_1 - NATURAL LANGUAGE PROCESSING
emails.body:
Hi all,

Please see the following NLP related internship opportunity.

Hello Daniel,

My company runs an internship program for Masters students and qualified undergraduates which started today. Unfortunately, three of our interns dropped out of the program at the last second leaving us with some available spots. Please let me know if you would be willing to send this to a few of your students and/or colleagues as we are looking to replace these interns as soon as possible.

Attached is a copy of a plan and timeline on what the interns will be doing this summer.

The main theme is that we are a consumer facing Fintech building out our Gen AI capabilities with a plan to create a resource for consumers that replaces expensive financial advisors and supplements the workflows of CPA's/CFA's/and wealth managers.

For more information about our company you can visit our website @ [https://www.musetax.com] (https://www.musetax.com/) or reach out to me with any questions.

About the internship:

*

* Starting as soon as possible

* Fully remote capabilities (company based in NYC flatiron so we do operate on Eastern Standard Time)

Qualified applicants will:

*

* Have interest in and experience fine-tuning LLM's

* Have interest in and experience using AWS sagemaker, Hugging Face, and Bedrock

* Have interest in and experience creating and implementing AWS architecture

* Strong proficiency in Python and Java

* Experience working in an Agile software development framework

* Experience or course work dealing with Data Structures

* Experience working with Git

* Great communication skills and work ethic

Any and all qualified applicants apply through the following link: [https://forms.gle/WiR4t9Bn37ScbPnj9] (https://forms.gle/WiR4t9Bn37ScbPnj9)

Thank you for considering this request. If you have any questions or would like further information, please don't hesitate to reach out.

Best regards,
Sam Larson
Machine Learning Team Lead @ Muse Tax

Hi Karthik,

Thanks for participating in the 2024 Barclays Associate Program recruitment process.

Hiring manager Ayush Garg from Acquisition & Advanced Analytics - Partner (Non-Air) & Portfolio Analytics team is impressed by your interview performance and think you might be a good fit for the team.
We have provided this positive feedback to our vendor Randstad who handles our internship contracts.
Please provide email confirmation that you would accept the internship from us, then we will continue the process with you and you should expect to receive the contract from Randstad.

Please let us know your final decision by Wednesday, April 17th. However, it would be greatly appreciated if you could respond back before Wednesday, April 10th so that we can expedite the onboarding process for you.

Contract duration: 7 months (Jun’24 – Dec’24)
Primary working location: 745 7th Ave, New York, NY
Working hours: 40 hours max in the summer and 20 hours max during Fall semester (hybrid – 60% in the office, 40% WFH)
Hourly rate: $40.50

We are happy to arrange a call by request to discuss any question you may have regarding this internship. Looking forward to hear back from you soon!

Best,
Isaac

emails.subject: This Week at Columbia: Events & Opportunities
emails.body:
[View in your browser](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/view?reset=1&id=562&cid=6&cs=d19d437e20e597e8e9b7094da9598bd1_1762897374_168  )

[University Life website](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=14066&qid=4524  )

Dear Students,

We hope you can take a break from studying and join us at some of the events below to unwind and connect with others.

Community Citizenship Initiative

The events below are designed to help students fulfill their [Community Citizenship Initiative](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=14067&qid=4524  ) (CCI) participation requirements. CCI is required for all newly enrolled students and optional for others.

http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=14067&qid=4524  

Orientalism, Enlightenment, and Human Endarkenment

Wednesday, November 12
2:30 - 4:00 p.m.
In-Person

Join us for a lecture exploring how Orientalism is not just prejudice of West against East, but also functions at a deep structural level and stems from an epistemology that constituted the Enlightenment itself. It argues that in all our systems of modern life, Orientalism is philosophically and materially anti-human. [Register here](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=14068&qid=4524  ).

Islamaphobia and the Challenge of Inclusion

Times Vary
In-Person

These sessions will explore definitions of Islamophobia, its manifestations, and the distinctions between interpersonal and structural forms. Participants will come away with increased awareness and knowledge of Islamophobia and how to recognize it, and their role in creating an inclusive campus environment.

- Monday, November 17, 10:00 - 11:30 a.m. [Register here](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=14069&qid=4524  ).
- Monday, November 17, 1:30 - 3:00 p.m. [Register here](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=14070&qid=4524  ).

Leading and Engaging in Society – Creating Positive Change

Times Vary
In-Person

Reflect on your impact as a global citizen and an agent of positive social change in this workshop geared to help you strengthen your skills in empathy, collaboration, and community engagement.

- Thursday, November 20, 3:00 - 4:30 p.m. [Register here](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=14071&qid=4524  ).
- Friday, November 21, 11:00 a.m. - 12:30 p.m. [Register here](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=14072&qid=4524  ).

Jewish Identity and Antisemitism

Times VaryIn-Person

Through interactive discussions and reflection, these workshops explore the roots and modern realities of Jewish identity and antisemitism. Participants will leave with a deeper understanding of Jewish identity, practical tools for addressing antisemitism, and resources for continued learning.

- Tuesday, December 2, 12:00 - 1:30 p.m. [Register here](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=14073&qid=4524  ).
- Thursday, December 4, 3:30 - 5:00 p.m. [Register here](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=14074&qid=4524  ).
- Monday, December 8, 2:00 - 3:30 p.m. [Register here](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=14075&qid=4524  ).
- Tuesday, December 9, 12:00 - 1:30 p.m. [Register here](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=14076&qid=4524  ).

University Life Events & Opportunities

All events listed below are in Eastern Time (ET), free, and open to all students across all schools and campuses.

http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=14078&qid=4524  

Drop-In Hours: Earl Hall

Today, November 11 | 3:00 - 6:00 p.m.
Hybrid

Stop by [Earl Hall](http://communications.universitylife.columbia.edu/civicrm/mailing/url?u=14079&qid=4524  ) or r

emails.subject: Columbia CDL HCI-AI Research Interest
emails.body:
Hi Karthik,

I hope this email finds you well. My name is Humad Bari and I am a first-year junior transfer student at UCLA, intending to declare a major in Linguistics & Computer Science. I was fascinated by your paper Simulating Cooperative Prosocial Behavior with Multi-Agent LLMs: Evidence and Mechanisms for AI Agents to Inform Policy Decisions, and how it leverages multi-agent LLM systems to replicate human prosocial behavior and explore unbounded actions to inform policy design. My academic goal is to pursue a Ph.D. in Human-Computer Interaction, focusing on human-AI, social media, and behavioral interaction. I was wondering if there might be any way I could volunteer towards your research, as I deeply admire the HCI work you’re doing.
This past summer, I co-authored a Human-AI interaction paper under review at ACM CHI 2026 with Professor Anthony Chen at UCLA, analyzing UX professionals’ interactions with GenAI tools. I am eager to apply my experience with human-AI research to design and evaluate human-robot interactions. I have beginner-to-intermediate experience with Python programming, a solid foundation in statistical analysis (including ANOVA and t-tests), and a strong understanding of user-centered design principles. I also have very proficient experience applying thematic analysis and other qualitative research methods to examine user behavioral patterns.
I would be grateful for the opportunity to voluntarily assist in your research in any capacity. I have attached my resume for your consideration and would be happy to provide any additional information.
Thank you very much for your time.
Best regards,
Humad Bari
hbari1@ucla.edu
(949) 278-4930
